skill.name: vue-best-practices
skill.description: Guides AI coding assistants through Vue 3 best practices, Composition API patterns, TypeScript usage, SSR pitfalls, performance tuning, and common framework gotchas.
skill.summary: Apply Vue 3 best practices for Composition API, TypeScript, SSR, performance, and maintainable components.
skill.author: vuejs-ai

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many critical and high patterns, but reviewed samples show they are markdown documentation and labeled examples, not executable skill code. The most severe examples describe XSS, SSR state leaks, filesystem access, browser storage, WebSocket use, and dynamic imports for educational Vue guidance. No prompt injection attempt or malicious operational intent was found, so the remaining publication risk is low.

low_findings.title: Educational XSS Payload Examples
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE for malicious intent. The file shows cookie theft, keylogging, and phishing payload examples inside a section named XSS Attack Examples, after warning users not to use v-html with untrusted content. This content is risky if copied blindly, but it is clearly framed as defensive Vue security guidance.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The dangerous strings are present, but the surrounding file explicitly labels them as attack examples and recommends sanitization or text interpolation. This strongly supports educational intent rather than credential theft.

low_findings.title: Static Code Execution Alerts Are Markdown Backticks
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE. The external command findings are caused by Markdown backticks around Vue syntax and JavaScript snippets, not Ruby or shell execution. No evidence found that the skill invokes commands or executes these examples.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited locations contain prose, inline code formatting, and checklist text. They are not executable files and do not define command execution behavior.

low_findings.title: Network and Storage Examples Are Contextual Guidance
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE for hidden network behavior. The network, WebSocket, fetch, and browser storage references appear in examples about Vue lifecycle cleanup, SSR guards, and v-html risk. No evidence found that the skill performs outbound requests itself.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The examples are embedded in markdown lessons and are paired with cleanup, naming, or XSS explanations. This makes active network exfiltration unlikely, though the examples contain real risky API names.

dangerous_patterns.title: Dynamic Imports in Vue Lazy Loading Examples
dangerous_patterns.description: FALSE_POSITIVE. Dynamic import expressions are used to explain Vue Router lazy loading and async components. They are ordinary Vue code-splitting examples, not obfuscated module loading by the skill.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The cited examples are inside a guide contrasting bad and good route loading patterns. The imports reference local Vue view files, not remote code.

dangerous_patterns.title: Filesystem Access in SSR Platform Example
dangerous_patterns.description: FALSE_POSITIVE. Node.js fs usage appears in a guide about avoiding platform-specific APIs in universal SSR code. One example is explicitly marked wrong for universal code, and the safe example moves filesystem work to server-only code.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The fs calls are documentation examples and include explanatory labels. There is no evidence the skill reads local files or secrets at runtime.

dangerous_patterns.title: SSR State Leak Example Is Defensive Guidance
dangerous_patterns.description: TRUE_POSITIVE as a security topic, FALSE_POSITIVE as marketplace malware. The SSR guide intentionally describes cross-request state pollution and gives safer per-request state patterns. This is a real application risk being documented, not malicious skill behavior.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The guide states the risk directly and provides mitigations with Pinia and per-request factories. The context confirms defensive intent.

content.value_statement: Vue projects often fail through subtle reactivity, SSR, typing, and performance mistakes. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code targeted Vue guidance with practical checks and safe patterns.
actual_capabilities: Recommends Composition API and script setup patterns for Vue 3 components.
Explains Vue reactivity pitfalls involving refs, reactive objects, computed values, and watchers.
Guides TypeScript usage for props, emits, template refs, directives, and provide or inject patterns.
Identifies SSR risks such as browser-only APIs and cross-request state pollution.
Provides performance guidance for lists, KeepAlive, transitions, computed stability, and rendering choices.
Covers Vue Router, Pinia, Vite, Suspense, Teleport, slots, directives, and render functions.
limitations: It is a reference skill and does not run Vue tooling or inspect a project automatically.
Examples are general guidance and may need adaptation to an existing codebase style.
Some guidance targets Vue 3 and may not apply to Vue 2 projects without migration work.
It does not replace security review for production SSR, authentication, or user content handling.

use_cases.title: Review Vue Components
use_cases.description: Check Vue components for reactivity, computed, watcher, prop, emit, and template mistakes before merging changes.

use_cases.title: Plan Vue 3 Migration
use_cases.description: Guide a team from Options API or Vue 2 patterns toward Composition API, script setup, and typed component contracts.

use_cases.title: Harden SSR Behavior
use_cases.description: Find SSR problems involving shared state, browser-only APIs, hydration, route loading, and request isolation.

prompt_templates.title: Check a Component
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner review of a single Vue file
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this Vue component for Vue 3 best practices. Focus on refs, computed properties, watchers, props, emits, and template safety.

prompt_templates.title: Refactor to Composition API
prompt_templates.prompt: Refactor this Vue component toward Composition API with script setup. Preserve behavior and explain any reactivity or TypeScript changes.

prompt_templates.title: Diagnose Performance
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this Vue feature for performance problems. Check computed stability, list rendering, transitions, KeepAlive usage, and watcher scope.

prompt_templates.title: Audit SSR Safety
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced review for server-rendered Vue apps
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit this Vue SSR code for browser-only APIs, shared request state, hydration risks, async component loading, and secure user data isolation.

output_examples.input: A component mutates a prop and sorts a reactive array inside a computed value.
output: Flags prop mutation as a one-way data flow issue.
Explains that sorting the reactive array mutates source state.
Suggests emitting an update and sorting a copied array instead.

output_examples.input: A Vue SSR composable reads localStorage during setup.
output: Identifies the server crash risk from browser-only APIs.
Recommends a safe default for server render.
Moves storage access behind an onMounted or window guard.

output_examples.input: A route config wraps route components with defineAsyncComponent.
output: Explains that Vue Router already supports route-level lazy loading.
Recommends direct dynamic imports for route components.
Suggests navigation guards or global indicators for route loading state.

best_practices: Prefer Composition API with script setup and TypeScript unless the project requires Options API.
Use computed values for pure derived state and watchers for targeted side effects.
Treat SSR code as universal code and guard browser-only or server-only APIs explicitly.
anti_patterns: Do not render untrusted user content with v-html without sanitization.
Do not mutate props, computed return values, or source arrays inside computed getters.
Do not create module-level singleton state for SSR request-specific user data.

faq.question: Does this skill work with Vue 2?
faq.answer: It mainly targets Vue 3. Some migration guidance applies to Vue 2, but the default advice assumes Vue 3.

faq.question: Does it require TypeScript?
faq.answer: No. It strongly recommends TypeScript for maintainable Vue projects, but many patterns also apply to JavaScript.

faq.question: Can it fix code automatically?
faq.answer: It can guide Claude, Codex, or Claude Code to propose and apply changes when those tools have repository access.

faq.question: Does it include security guidance?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers Vue-specific issues such as v-html XSS risk, SSR state leaks, and browser API misuse.

faq.question: Is it tied to Nuxt?
faq.answer: No. It includes Nuxt-related notes where relevant, but the guidance is for Vue applications broadly.

faq.question: Will it run tests or linters?
faq.answer: No. The skill provides guidance. The host assistant must run project tools separately when available.
